input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install the .NET SDK (if not already installed)

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# If missing, install via winget:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# See available workloads and their versions dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows, iOS sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify it installed
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI3/Windows App SDK)

The Windows head requires the Windows App SDK, which ships through VS components:

```powershell
# Option A — full Visual Studio (recommended)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then add required workload components via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --norestart

# Option B — Build Tools only (CI / no IDE)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

## 4. Set up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload auto-installs an Android SDK, but you can verify/manage it:

```powershell
# Discover where the Android SDK landed
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkPath"

# Or check the default location:
ls "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
ls "$env:ProgramFiles(x86)\Android\android-sdk" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

# If you need to manually set it:
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")

# List installed SDK components dynamically:
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install/update specific platform APIs if needed:
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## 5. Accept Android licenses

```powershell
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
# Answer 'y' to all prompts
```

## 6. Set up an Android emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Install an emulator image
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

# Create an AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 7. Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for WinUI3/packaged app deployment
Start-Process ms-settings:developers
# Then toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the UI

# Or via registry (requires admin):
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 8. Create and verify a project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (ensure emulator is running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## 9. Diagnose issues

```powershell
# All-in-one health check — shows missing components
dotnet workload repair
maui-check   # if you installed the global tool below

# Optional: install the MAUI diagnostic tool
dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check
maui-check
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0-*` with your actual SDK major version (`dotnet --version` to check).
- The Windows TFM version (`windows10.0.19041.0`) must match what's in your `.csproj` — the template sets this automatically.
- Hyper-V or WHPX should be enabled for Android emulator acceleration (`bcdedit /set hypervisorlaunchtype auto` then reboot).
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
